result.summary: getmaxim.ai exposes a documented REST API at https://api.getmaxim.ai, a separate OTLP HTTP ingestion endpoint at https://api.getmaxim.ai/v1/otel, and a downloadable `maxim` CLI; all require a Maxim-issued API key, and OTLP also requires a Maxim repository ID header.
result.description: Maxim AI is a platform for evaluating, testing, and observing AI agents and applications. It provides prompt management, datasets, workflows, test runs, alerting, and tracing/observability features for generative AI systems.

maxim_api_key.label: Maxim API key
maxim_api_key.setup: In the Maxim web app, go to [Maxim API keys](https://www.getmaxim.ai/docs/settings/maxim-api-keys) or navigate to `Settings` → `API keys`, click `Generate new`, give the key a name, and copy the generated value. Use this key in the `x-maxim-api-key` header for HTTP APIs, or set `MAXIM_API_KEY` for the CLI.

maxim_repo_id.label: Maxim log repository ID
maxim_repo_id.setup: Create or identify the target log repository in your Maxim workspace, then copy its repository ID. The OTLP ingestion docs show this value must be sent alongside your API key as the `x-maxim-repo-id` header when posting traces to Maxim's OTLP endpoint. See [Ingesting via OTLP Endpoint](https://www.getmaxim.ai/docs/tracing/opentelemetry/ingesting-via-otlp).
